rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND VINCENT DIGIACOMO JR VS. SHRYNE GROUP, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANT VIGILANT EYE SECURITY, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Continued to March 3, 2025 on the court's own motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MISAEL ROSAS FRAGA, INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF VS. ANCHOR BREWING COMPANY, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2025, Line 3. 1 - FINAL APPROVAL HEARING. Plaintiff's unopposed motion for final approval of class and PAGA action settlement is continued to March 12, 2025. Although Plaintiff's moving papers refer to the Declaration of Cassandra Polites of ILYM Group, Inc., that declaration does not appear to have been filed. Based on the moving papers, this declaration appears to supply information upon personal knowledge about the actual mailing of notice to class members and any responses; it is information the court requires before approval may be granted. The court orders Plaintiff to file the missing declaration forthwith and by no later than March 5, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: XIZHEN ZHU ET AL VS. KINGDOM OF DUMPLING LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Xizhen Zhu To Attend A Deposition, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT KINGDOM OF DUMPLING LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Xizhen Zhu To Attend A Deposition. Defendant Kingdom of Dumpling LLC moves to compel Plaintiff Xizhen Zhu to attend a deposition and for sanctions. The unopposed motion is GRANTED. The Jew declaration establishes that Kingdom noticed Zhu's deposition for January 31, 2025. Zhu's counsel stated she would not be produced for deposition. The court finds Jew's meet and confer efforts reasonable. The court finds good cause for defendant Kingdom to depose plaintiff Zhu in this case and orders Zhu to attend a deposition, scheduled on a date convenient to the parties, within 10 court days of notice of entry of this order. Kingdom further seeks sanctions. CCP 2025.450(g) provides that if a motion to compel a deponent to attend a deposition is granted, "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . in favor of the party who noticed the deposition and against the deponent or the party with whom the deponent is affiliated, unless the court finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court does not find substantial justification here. The court orders Zhu to pay $2250 to Kingdom within 10 court days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: VADIM KOGAN VS. UWE GUEHNEMANN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Upon Stipulation Releasing $10,000 Writ Of Attachment Cash Bond With Court Clerk And $21,864.94 Held By San Francisco Sheriff Pursuant To Issued Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2025, Line 4. PLAINTIFF VADIM KOGAN's Motion For Order Upon Stipulation Releasing $10,000 Writ Of Attachment Cash Bond With Court Clerk And $21,864.94 Held By San Francisco Sheriff Pursuant To Issued Writ Of Attachment. The parties have settled this matter and have stipulated to two orders. With this motion, they seek entry of the orders. No opposition was filed. Pursuant to the terms of the parties' settlement, and for good cause shown in the moving papers, IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that the San Francisco County Sheriff shall disburse the amount of funds held under writ of attachment at the San Francisco County Sheriff under its Sheriff's File No. 23476111, representing the principal sum of $21,864.94 attached at JP Morgan Chase Bank with account number ending in xxx5225, account of DEFCO, UWE GUEHNEMANN, plus any interest accrued thereon, less any Sheriff's costs, to Plaintiff Vadim Kogan through his attorney of record. Said funds shall be mailed by the Sheriff to Plaintiff's Attorney, Mark J. Rice, Esq., McNeil, Silveira, Rice & Wiley, 55 Professional Center Parkway, Suite A, San Rafael, CA 94903, as part of the settlement of the above case. Upon such payment the writ of attachment shall be discharged. IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that this court's treasurer and/or disbursing officer shall issue its warrant or check made payable to Plaintiff Vadim Kogan in the cash sum of $10,000 in principal, plus any applicable earned interest thereon, as and for the return to Plaintiff of the Deposit of Undertaking, Transaction Number H2423C21D008, Tag #037485, deposited with the court by Plaintiff Kogan on December 21, 2023. The court shall mail said funds to Plaintiff's attorney, Mark J. Rice, McNeil, Silveira, Rice & Wiley, 55 Professional Center Parkway, Suite A, San Rafael, CA 94903. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: PERRY ZHANG ET AL VS. SPROUTS FARMERS MARKET, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc Responses To Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2025, Line 7. PLAINTIFFS PERRY ZHANG, AND YAN DUAN's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc Responses To Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, Request For Production Of Documents. Plaintiffs Perry Zhang and Yan Duan seek to compel further discovery from defendant Sprouts Farmers Market, Inc., concerning three categories of information: incidents at other Sprouts stores where vehicles drove onto pedestrian walkways; bollards at other Sprouts stores; and insurance discounts for having bollards or vehicle barriers at stores. As discussed below, the motion is granted in part and denied in part. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH HSU VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery calendar for Thursday, February 27, 2025, Line 5. 2 - DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant the Regents of the University of California move to strike references to punitive damages in Plaintiff Elizabeth Hsu's Third Amended Complaint (TAC). The court grants the motion, striking the prayer for punitive damages at 5:12 of the TAC. The only defendants named in the complaint are the Regents, a governmental entity that administers the University of California. A public entity is not liable for punitive damages. (Gov. Code section 818.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.)
